title: tui/event.rs: /\! command uses blocking std::process::Command — freezes Tokio executor
description: In tui/src/event.rs handle_slash(), the '\!' command (line 1109) calls std::process::Command::new(&argv[0]).output() — the synchronous, blocking version from std::process. This runs inside an async fn on a Tokio cooperative task. It blocks the Tokio thread until the subprocess exits, which freezes: (1) the 50ms render tick, (2) relay message reception, (3) all keyboard input. If the subprocess is slow (e.g. 'sleep 10'), the UI is completely unresponsive for that duration. The cli version correctly uses tokio::process::Command::new(...).output().await. Fix: replace std::process::Command with tokio::process::Command and .await the result.
